mcqs.question: What is the main difference between DFA and NFA?

options.text: DFA is faster than NFA
NFA is more convenient to design than DFA
DFA has a unique next state for each input symbol, while NFA has multiple next states
NFA is more efficient than DFA

mcqs.explanation: The main difference between DFA and NFA is that DFA has a unique next state for each input symbol, while NFA has multiple next states.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of using NFA?

options.text: To recognize patterns in strings
To parse complex grammars
To optimize DFA
To reduce the number of states in DFA

mcqs.explanation: NFA is used to recognize patterns in strings, especially when it is more convenient to design a Finite Automata with multiple next states for a single input symbol.

mcqs.question: What is the concept of epsilon transitions in NFA?

options.text: Transitions that can be taken without consuming any input symbol
Transitions that can be taken only when the input symbol is epsilon
Transitions that can be taken only when the input symbol is not epsilon
Transitions that cannot be taken at all

mcqs.explanation: Epsilon transitions are transitions that can be taken without consuming any input symbol, allowing the NFA to move to a new state without reading any input.

mcqs.question: How does NFA handle ambiguity?

options.text: By rejecting the string if there is ambiguity
By accepting the string if any of the possible paths lead to an accepting state
By using a different algorithm to resolve the ambiguity
By ignoring the ambiguity

mcqs.explanation: NFA can handle ambiguity by allowing multiple possible next states for a single input symbol, and then resolving the ambiguity by accepting the string if any of the possible paths lead to an accepting state.

mcqs.question: What is the relationship between NFA and DFA?

options.text: Every NFA can be converted to an equivalent DFA, but not every DFA can be converted to an equivalent NFA
Every DFA can be converted to an equivalent NFA, but not every NFA can be converted to an equivalent DFA
NFA and DFA are equivalent in terms of the languages they can recognize
NFA is more powerful than DFA

mcqs.explanation: Every NFA can be converted to an equivalent DFA, but not every DFA can be converted to an equivalent NFA.
